mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the Industrial Production Index captures real-time manufacturing output and capacity utilization. Positive momentum in industrial activity directly precedes upward earnings revisions, causing the S&P 500 to generate positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.5 percent, the zero lower bound constraint forces investors to reach for yield in risk assets, compressing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirm a synchronized recovery across labor and goods-producing sectors. This joint signal reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of PAYEMS and INDPRO jointly fails to achieve statistical significance at the 5 percent level in predicting 20-day returns, or the combined signal underperforms single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal a bottoming in residential investment and a revival in construction-dependent demand. This leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations reflate.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in HOUST during post-shock recovery regimes coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms economy-wide final demand expansion. Higher nominal output translates directly into top-line revenue growth for the S&P 500 constituent firms, generating positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly GDPC1 growth does not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55 percent of observations during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation drops below 1.0 percent year-over-year, markets price elevated deflation risk and expect prolonged monetary accommodation. The resulting decline in real interest rates boosts present equity values, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 1.0 percent show average negative 20-day S&P 500 returns during out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate and the consumer price index indicate a stagflationary regime where monetary tightening coincides with rising input costs. Corporate profit margins contract and the S&P 500 delivers negative returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint upward moves in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fail to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observed episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month jump in the unemployment rate larger than 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration ahead of consensus recognition. Risk assets reprice downward and the S&P 500 generates negative returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month.
